title: Review the repo for risks/regressions around implementing a global slash-command fallback path. Focus on current prompt contract in AGENTS/CODEX/README/tests, existing launcher scripts, and where a change would need regression coverage. Return findings and a safer implementation boundary. Do not edi...
summary_text: **Findings (ordered by risk)** 1. **High: CI won’t reliably run adapter/launcher regressions for launcher-only changes.** Path filters in the gate workflow include prompt/docs/tests, but not the launcher sources themselves, while the jobs that would catch regressions are inside that same workflow. Evidence: [fugue-orchestration-gate.yml#L5](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml#L5), [fugue-orchestration-gate.yml#L256](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml#L256). Impact: a global slash-fallback change in launcher paths can merge without executing the relevant tests. 2. **High: current `kernel` launcher can silently fall through to prompt launch, bypassing guard authority and runtime evidence.** Launcher flow ends at prompt fallback if open/new/guard paths don’t execute. Evidence: [scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L50](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L50), [scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L53](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L53). Contract pressure: guard is documented as execution authority and minimum-lane enforcement boundary. Evidence: [CODEX.md#L95](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/CODEX.md#L95), [CODEX.md#L97](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/CODEX.md#L97), [.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L56](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L56). This is the key single-lane fallback violation risk if fallback scope is widened. 3. **Medium: installer and launcher dependency contract are mismatched.** Installer deploys only `kernel`/`k4` + prompt symlinks/snippet; `kernel` launcher expects sibling executables (`k`, `kernel-root`, `codex-kernel-guard`, `codex-prompt-launch`, etc.). Evidence: [install-kernel-launchers.sh#L162](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh#L162), [scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L21](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L21), [scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L47](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L47), [scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L53](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L53). Coverage gap: install test validates files/symlinks, not end-to-end execution of installed launcher in a clean host state. Evidence: [test-install-kernel-launchers.sh#L44](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-install-kernel-launchers.sh#L44). 4. **Medium: global `codex` wrapper interception is broad and brittle for new/flag-first CLI forms.** Anything not in a small bypass list routes to `kernel` when `kernel-root` resolves. Evidence: [codex-orchestrator.zsh#L47](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/codex-orchestrator.zsh#L47), [codex-orchestrator.zsh#L65](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/codex-orchestrator.zsh#L65). Coverage currently checks only a narrow subset (`exec`, missing root). Evidence: [test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh#L50](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh#L50). 5. **Medium: `/k` is declared equivalent to `/kernel`, but prompt content has drift.** Contract says `/k` must obey `/kernel` bootstrap contract; current files differ beyond alias intro. Evidence: [CODEX.md#L94](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/CODEX.md#L94), [.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L100](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L100), [.codex/prompts/k.md#L99](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/k.md#L99). This weakens confidence if fallback swaps between `/kernel` and `/k`. **Safer implementation boundary** 1. Keep fallback **repo-local and command-specific**: only unresolved `/kernel` and `/k` paths in this repo context; no wildcard/global...
